engineGuide.h2: Peugeot RCZ Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Peugeot RCZ across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol Turbo
entries.history: The entry-level THP unit delivering 156hp; defined the RCZ's accessible performance character but inherited timing chain concerns.
compatibleVariants: THP 156, THP 156 Allure, THP 156 GT, Asphalt Edition, Carbon Edition
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear at 60k-90k miles - causes rattling on cold start

entries.title: pressure fuel pump (HPFP) failure leading to misfire and loss of power
entries.cta: Get quotes for EP6CDT Peugeot RCZ engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol Turbo
entries.history: The mid-range THP producing 200hp; balanced performance and drivability, most popular RCZ engine for UK enthusiasts.
compatibleVariants: THP 200, THP 200 GT, Red Carbon Edition, Magnetic Edition, Asphalt Edition (late)
commonFailures: HPFP failure under high-load driving - typical at 70k-100k miles
Turbo wastegate actuator sticking causing boost pressure faults
Carbon buildup on intake valves reducing power and fuel economy
entries.cta: Get quotes for EP6CDTX Peugeot RCZ engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol Turbo
entries.history: The flagship THP 270 Peugeot Sport unit; highest specific output 1.6L production engine at launch, built for track-capable performance.
compatibleVariants: THP 270 Peugeot Sport, RCZ R, Ultimate Edition

entries.title: stress connecting rod bearing wear under track use - check at 50k+ miles
spec timing components requiring specialist inspection

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel Turbo
entries.history: The sole diesel option for the RCZ; offered strong torque and economy but suffered EGR-related issues common to PSA HDi units.
commonFailures: EGR cooler carbon clogging causing reduced power and warning lights
DPF regeneration issues on short-journey driving patterns
Turbo variable vane sticking at higher mileage

commonProblems.h2: Peugeot RCZ Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: Peugeot RCZ Timing Chain Failure - Why THP Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot RCZ THP 156, THP 200, THP 270 (2010-2015, EP6CDT/EP6CDTX/EP6CDTR)
problems.rootCause: The rear-mounted timing chain tensioner loses hydraulic pressure over time, allowing chain stretch that risks valve timing loss and catastrophic interference damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, guides before failure; requires engine front cover removal
repairOptions.longevity: Adds 40k-60k miles if caught early; not viable once rattling is severe

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chain replacement plus valve clearance check; may require head removal if timing jumped
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if no valve contact occurred; compression test essential first

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt THP engine with upgraded tensioner, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage RCZ or if valve damage confirmed; eliminates recurrence risk

problems.recommendation: On a Peugeot RCZ worth £4,000-£12,000, preventative chain replacement at 60k miles offers excellent value. If rattling is already present or compression is low, a specialist rebuilt engine with upgraded components is often more cost-effective than extensive head repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EP6CDT/EP6CDTX engine prices for your Peugeot RCZ

problems.h4: Peugeot RCZ HPFP Failure - High-Pressure Fuel Pump Issues
problems.rootCause: The high-pressure fuel pump's internal cam follower wears, causing low rail pressure, misfires, and eventual engine limp mode or no-start conditions.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Swap HPFP unit with calibrated replacement; requires fuel system depressurisation
repairOptions.longevity: Restores function if no collateral damage; typical lifespan 40k-60k miles on replacement

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: HPFP replacement plus injector flow testing and ECU adaptation reset
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if misfires persisted; ensures full system calibration

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Upgraded HPFP
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt THP engine with reinforced HPFP drive components and warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage RCZ or if fuel contamination damaged other components

problems.recommendation: HPFP failure on a THP 200/270 is often a standalone issue. If the engine runs smoothly otherwise, a specialist HPFP replacement at £600-£950 is sensible. For RCZs over 100k miles with other wear signs, factor in a rebuilt engine quote for long-term peace of mind.
problems.cta: Get quotes for EP6CDTX Peugeot RCZ HPFP replacement or full engine rebuild

problems.group: Peugeot RCZ Turbo Oil Feed Pipe Leak
problems.h4: Peugeot RCZ Turbo Oil Feed Pipe Leak - Common THP Issue
problems.rootCause: The factory crimped turbo oil feed pipe develops leaks at the banjo union due to thermal cycling and vibration, causing oil loss, smoke, and potential turbo bearing damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace factory pipe with braided stainless hose and AN fittings; includes new seals
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix for the leak point; does not address turbo wear if already damaged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, replace bearings/seals, fit upgraded oil feed; bench-tested before refit
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if turbo shows shaft play or bearing noise; extends turbo life significantly

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Upgraded Turbo System
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt THP engine with upgraded turbo oil system and 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if turbo failure caused engine oil starvation or if RCZ has high mileage

problems.recommendation: The braided hose upgrade at £300-£500 is excellent preventative maintenance on any THP RCZ. If the turbo is already noisy or the engine has suffered oil starvation, a specialist rebuilt engine with upgraded components offers better long-term reliability than turbo-only repairs.
problems.cta: Compare Peugeot RCZ THP turbo repair vs rebuilt engine costs

problems.group: Peugeot RCZ Carbon Buildup on Intake Valves
problems.h4: Peugeot RCZ Carbon Buildup on Intake Valves - Direct Injection Issue
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot RCZ THP 156, THP 200, THP 270 (All EP6 THP variants)
problems.rootCause: Direct injection petrol engines lack fuel washing over intake valves, allowing carbon deposits from PCV vapours to accumulate, reducing airflow and causing rough idle, misfires, and power loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove intake manifold, blast valves with walnut media to remove carbon; no engine removal
repairOptions.longevity: Restores power and idle quality; recommended every 40k-50k miles as preventative maintenance

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Walnut blasting plus new intake/exhaust gaskets, throttle body clean, ECU adaptation reset
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if carbon caused persistent fault codes or rough running after basic clean

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Updated Intake Design
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt THP engine with revised intake components and warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only justified if carbon buildup caused severe valve damage or if engine has other major wear

problems.recommendation: Walnut blasting at £250-£450 is cost-effective preventative maintenance for any THP RCZ over 40k miles. It's far cheaper than engine replacement and restores performance. Only consider a rebuilt engine if carbon caused physical valve damage confirmed by compression test.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot RCZ THP walnut blasting service or engine replacement

problems.h4: Peugeot RCZ Wastegate Actuator Failure - Turbo Boost Issues
problems.rootCause: The turbo wastegate actuator diaphragm or linkage wears, causing incorrect boost pressure control, reduced power, and fault codes (P0299/P0234).

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace wastegate actuator with calibrated unit; requires turbo access but not removal
repairOptions.longevity: Restores boost control if turbo internals are sound; typical lifespan 40k-60k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, replace bearings/seals/vanes, fit new actuator; bench-tested before refit
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if turbo shows shaft play, bearing noise, or oil leaks alongside actuator fault

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Upgraded Turbo
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt THP engine with upgraded turbo assembly and 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage RCZ or if turbo failure caused engine damage from oil starvation

problems.recommendation: Wastegate actuator failure is often isolated. If the turbo is otherwise healthy, actuator replacement at £400-£750 is sensible. For RCZs with other turbo symptoms (oil leaks, shaft play), factor in a rebuilt engine quote for comprehensive reliability.
problems.cta: Compare Peugeot RCZ THP wastegate repair vs specialist rebuilt engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Peugeot RCZ
finalCta.paragraph: The Peugeot RCZ's THP engines are performance-focused but demand specialist attention when issues arise. EngineMarket connects you with UK Peugeot RCZ specialists who understand EP6CDT/EP6CDTX/EP6CDTR failure patterns. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original weak point, bench testing, and a Peugeotmum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare quotes from vetted suppliers and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Peugeot RCZ Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Peugeot RCZ and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a Peugeotmum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Peugeot RCZ Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant

items.title: Peugeot RCZ 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6-litre EP6 THP turbo petrol engine powered every Peugeot RCZ variant from launch in 2010 to end of production in 2015, delivering 156hp, 200hp or 270hp depending on tune and components. This compact, high-specific-output unit defined the RCZ's performance character but requires specialist attention to timing chain and HPFP maintenance for long-term reliability.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner wear at 60k-90k miles; HPFP failure causing misfire; turbo oil feed pipe leaks
items.cta: Compare Peugeot RCZ 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Peugeot RCZ 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre DW10 HDi turbo diesel was offered in the Peugeot RCZ from 2010-2014 as the sole diesel option, delivering 163hp with strong low-end torque and improved fuel economy versus the THP petrol units. While robust in design, EGR and DPF-related issues on short-journey driving patterns drive most replacement enquiries for this variant. Not sure which engine size your Peugeot RCZ needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a Peugeotmum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Peugeot RCZ specialists.
commonFailurePoints: EGR cooler carbon clogging; DPF regeneration issues on urban use; turbo variable vane sticking
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot RCZ 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot RCZ Petrol Engines

items.descriptor: The 1.6L EP6 THP turbo petrol engine powered every Peugeot RCZ variant from 2010-2015, with three power outputs defining the model's performance range.
items.familiesLabel: Common Peugeot RCZ Petrol Engine Families
families: EP6CDT - THP 156 entry-level unit; timing chain tensioner wear most common failure
EP6CDTX - THP 200 balanced performance; HPFP and turbo oil feed leaks frequent
EP6CDTR - THP 270 Peugeot Sport; high-stress bearing wear under track use
foundIn: Peugeot RCZ T75 (2010-2015) - EP6CDT, EP6CDTX, EP6CDTR
knownFor: Strong specific output and engaging throttle response across all THP tunes
Timing chain tensioner failures requiring preventative replacement at 60k-90k miles
High-pressure fuel pump (HPFP) failures causing misfire and limp mode
Turbo oil feed pipe leaks leading to smoke and potential bearing damage

engineYears.h2: Peugeot RCZ Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Peugeot RCZ's 2010-2015 production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The RCZ launched with two THP petrol engines: 156hp EP6CDT and 200hp EP6CDTX, defining the model's accessible performance character.
keyChanges: Peugeot RCZ production begins at Magna Steyr, Austria
EP6CDT (156hp) and EP6CDTX (200hp) THP engines introduced
6-speed manual standard; 6-speed auto optional on THP 156
knownFor: Engaging handling and distinctive double-bubble roof design
Early EP6CDT/EP6CDTX units show timing chain tensioner wear by 60k miles
HPFP failures begin appearing on THP 200 models under high-load use
Strong enthusiast following drives active UK owner forum support
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010 Peugeot RCZ replacement engines

years.description: Peugeot expanded the RCZ range with limited editions (Asphalt, Red Carbon) while refining THP engine calibration for improved drivability.
keyChanges: Asphalt Edition (500 units) and Red Carbon Edition launched with THP 200
Minor ECU updates to improve throttle response and reduce turbo lag
Diesel 2.0 HDi 163 option introduced for UK market
mainEngines: EP6CDTX - 1.6 petrol turbo (THP 200, Asphalt, Red Carbon)
knownFor: Asphalt and Red Carbon editions command premium on used market due to limited numbers
DW10BTED4 diesel offers strong economy but EGR/DPF issues on urban driving
Timing chain tensioner failures now widely reported on early THP units
Specialist rebuilders begin offering upgraded tensioner kits for preventative replacement

keyChanges: Exterior refresh: new front bumper, LED DRLs, revised rear diffuser
THP 270 Peugeot Sport (EP6CDTR) introduced with 270hp, Torsen diff, upgraded brakes
Magnetic and Ultimate special editions launched with THP 200/270

Last RCZ rolls off Magna Steyr line in September 2015
No direct successor announced; RCZ remains a standalone performance coupe

faq.h2: Peugeot RCZ Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Peugeot RCZ engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Peugeot RCZ THP engines typically cost £1,800-£3,500 supply-only, while used units range £1,200-£2,400 depending on mileage and condition. Specialist supply & fit adds £400-£800 labour.
keyPoints: EP6CDT THP 156 rebuilt: £1,800-£2,600 supply-only
EP6CDTX THP 200 rebuilt: £2,200-£3,200 supply-only
EP6CDTR THP 270 rebuilt: £3,000-£4,500 supply-only
Used engines vary widely; verify compression and service history
Supply & fit labour typically £400-£800 at specialist garages
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Peugeot RCZ engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: What engine codes fit my Peugeot RCZ?
items.answer: Peugeot RCZ petrol engines use EP6CDT (THP 156), EP6CDTX (THP 200), or EP6CDTR (THP 270); diesel models use DW10BTED4 (2.0 HDi 163). Enter your reg for instant code verification.
keyPoints: EP6CDT: 156hp THP, 2010-2015, most common replacement code
EP6CDTX: 200hp THP, 2010-2015, popular performance variant
EP6CDTR: 270hp THP Peugeot Sport, 2014-2015, specialist rebuild required
DW10BTED4: 163hp diesel, 2010-2014, EGR/DPF issues common
DVLA lookup via registration confirms exact code for your RCZ
items.cta: Get quotes for your exact Peugeot RCZ engine code today

items.question: How long does Peugeot RCZ engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only rebuilt engines typically dispatch within 3-5 working days; specialist supply & fit installations take 1-2 days once the engine arrives at the garage.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine build time: 3-5 working days average
Delivery to UK mainland: 1-2 working days via courier
Specialist garage installation: 6-8 hours labour typically
Full supply & fit turnaround: 5-7 working days end-to-end
Express options available for urgent breakdown scenarios
items.cta: Find Peugeot RCZ specialist supply & fit availability near you

items.question: What warranty comes with Peugeot RCZ replacement engines?
items.answer: Rebuilt and reconditioned Peugeot RCZ engines supplied through EngineMarket include a Peugeotmum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour at any UK garage.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on rebuilt units
Warranty valid at any UK garage, not just the supplying specialist
Covers manufacturing defects and premature component failure
Excludes damage from incorrect installation or oil starvation
Used engine warranties typically shorter (3-6 months); confirm terms
items.cta: Compare Peugeot RCZ engine warranty terms from UK suppliers

items.question: Can I upgrade my Peugeot RCZ engine to a more powerful one?
items.answer: Upgrading from THP 156 to THP 200 or 270 is possible but requires ECU remapping, cooling upgrades, and specialist calibration; consult a Peugeot RCZ specialist before proceeding.
keyPoints: THP 156 to 200: feasible with ECU remap and minor hardware changes
THP 200 to 270: requires forged internals, upgraded turbo, cooling mods
ECU compatibility and immobiliser pairing essential for all swaps
Specialist calibration ensures emissions compliance and reliability
Budget £1,500-£4,000 beyond engine cost for full upgrade package
items.cta: Enquire about Peugeot RCZ THP performance upgrade compatibility

items.question: How do I know if my Peugeot RCZ needs a new engine or just repair?
items.answer: Get a compression test and leak-down diagnosis first; if compression is low across multiple cylinders or valves are bent, a rebuilt engine is often more cost-effective than extensive repairs.
keyPoints: Compression test: below 120psi on multiple cylinders suggests rebuild needed
Leak-down test identifies valve, ring, or head gasket issues specifically
Timing chain failure with valve contact usually requires full replacement
Isolated HPFP or turbo issues may be repairable without engine swap
Specialist diagnosis costs £80-£150 but prevents costly misdiagnosis
items.cta: Get a specialist diagnosis quote for your Peugeot RCZ engine issue

items.question: Are Peugeot RCZ replacement engines MOT compliant?
items.answer: Yes, all rebuilt and reconditioned Peugeot RCZ engines supplied through EngineMarket are Euro 5 compliant and include necessary certification for MOT emissions testing.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines retain original Euro 5 emissions certification
ECU calibration preserved to maintain emissions compliance
Documentation provided for MOT tester verification if required
Diesel units include DPF/EGR components where originally fitted
Non-compliant modifications void MOT; use OEM-spec rebuilds only
items.cta: Verify Peugeot RCZ engine MOT compliance before ordering

items.question: Do you supply Peugeot RCZ engines with gearbox?
items.answer: Engine-only supply is standard, but many specialists offer engine + gearbox packages for Peugeot RCZ; confirm availability and pricing when requesting quotes.
keyPoints: Supply-only engine pricing excludes transmission by default
Engine + 6-speed manual packages available from select suppliers
Automatic (Aisin) transmission compatibility requires verification
Clutch, flywheel, and mounts often recommended for concurrent replacement
Package deals can save £200-£400 versus separate component sourcing
items.cta: Compare Peugeot RCZ engine and gearbox package deals

items.question: Can I fit a Peugeot RCZ engine myself?
items.answer: Supply-only engines are available for DIY installation, but THP timing procedures and ECU adaptation require specialist tools; warranty validity may require professional fitting confirmation.
keyPoints: EP6 THP timing chain replacement demands precise locking tools
ECU adaptation reset requires Peugeot-specific diagnostic equipment
Torque specs and sealant procedures critical for long-term reliability
Some suppliers require professional fit confirmation for warranty validity
DIY saves labour cost but risks warranty and reliability if procedures missed
items.cta: Find Peugeot RCZ specialist fitting support for supply-only engines

items.question: What if my Peugeot RCZ engine code isn't listed?
items.answer: Contact EngineMarket specialists for rare or unlisted Peugeot RCZ engine codes; most EP6 variants can be sourced or adapted within 48 hours with expert verification.
keyPoints: Rare codes like 5FE/5FM may require cross-reference verification
Specialists can adapt compatible EP6 variants with ECU recalibration
Chassis/VIN verification ensures correct mounting and ancillary fitment
Lead time for rare codes: typically 2-5 working days for sourcing
Always confirm compatibility before ordering to avoid returns
items.cta: Get specialist help sourcing rare Peugeot RCZ engine codes
